codeReview.summary: Clean, correct implementation of SDF-based rounded rectangle fill with anti-aliased edges. The approach is sound: iterate corner arc rows, compute horizontal arc extent via fixed-point square root, write AA edge pixels with gamma-correct sRGB blending, fill solid interior with existing NEON-accelerated fill_rect. The fixed-point math (8.8 representation, isqrt_fp binary search) is correct — verified that x_arc_int never exceeds r after clamping, so no u32 underflow in left_solid computation. The top/bottom arc row overlap case (which would cause double-blending of AA pixels) is provably impossible after radius clamping to min(w,h)/2. Interior rows correctly delegate to fill_rect/fill_rect_blend, and the blend variant properly combines coverage with source alpha. All unsafe blocks have adequate SAFETY comments. 18 tests cover all 7 expectedBehavior items plus edge cases (clipping, zero dimensions, tiny rects, four-corner symmetry). Code follows existing drawing library patterns and reuses LUT infrastructure.

issues.description: rounded_rect_write_aa_pixel hardcodes PixelFormat::Bgra8888 for the fully-opaque fast path (line: `color.encode(PixelFormat::Bgra8888)`). This is consistent with fill_rect_blend_scalar_1px which also assumes BGRA layout, and the entire codebase uses Bgra8888 exclusively, but it's an implicit assumption that would break silently if another format were added. The surface's actual format (available via bpp parameter) isn't passed through.

fill_rounded_rect lacks the early-exit check `if x >= self.width || y >= self.height { return; }` that fill_rect and fill_rect_blend have. The code is still correct because each drawing path (arc rows, interior rect) has its own bounds checks, but an entirely off-screen rounded rect will iterate through all arc rows computing fixed-point math before each row's `if py >= surf_h { continue }` rejects it. Minor performance issue only — not a correctness bug.

isqrt_fp uses candidate*candidate comparison at each bit position (O(log n) multiplications). The classic Knuth/binary-digit method uses the (a+b)²=a²+2ab+b² identity to avoid full multiplication, running in O(log n) additions/shifts. The current approach is correct and adequate for the workload (called once per arc row, ~r times per rounded rect), but a more efficient algorithm exists if this becomes a hot path.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The drawing library uniformly assumes PixelFormat::Bgra8888 in all blending functions (fill_rect_blend_scalar_1px, neon_blend_const_4px, rounded_rect_write_aa_pixel). This is an implicit constraint not documented in AGENTS.md or architecture.md. If a second pixel format is ever added, multiple functions would need updating. Consider documenting 'Drawing library assumes Bgra8888 exclusively' as an explicit architectural constraint.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: fill_rect_blend_scalar_1px (lib.rs:1245) reads dst bytes in B,G,R,A order; rounded_rect_write_aa_pixel (lib.rs:1355) encodes with explicit PixelFormat::Bgra8888; neon_blend_const_4px (neon.rs:199) loads 4 BGRA pixels as u8x16.

summary: Pass. The rounded rectangle implementation is correct, well-structured, and thoroughly tested. SDF-based arc computation with fixed-point math is sound — verified no overflow/underflow edge cases. All 18 tests cover the full expectedBehavior specification including gamma-correct AA blending, radius clamping, NEON interior matching, and semi-transparent backgrounds. Three non-blocking observations noted: hardcoded Bgra8888 in AA pixel writer (consistent with codebase), missing early-exit for off-screen rects (perf only), and a more efficient isqrt algorithm exists. No blocking issues found.
